jokes.text: I bumped into madoff on the street one day and he was wearing this quilted jacket with the collar up. And for some reason it just creeped me out. So the next day I pulled all my money out.
jokes.setup: Dramatic context of losing everything to Madoff requires serious explanation
jokes.punchline: Million-dollar financial decision based on fashion choice creepiness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - avoiding financial ruin through superficial fashion judgment. Perfect Curb premise.

jokes.text: That's... That's my money! You have my money! Not according to the laws of the state of new york.
jokes.setup: George realizes his ex-wife has the money he lost
jokes.punchline: Cold legal technicality delivered with brutal efficiency
jokes.explanation: Sharp contrast between emotional desperation and cold legality. Great economy.

jokes.text: - I got it. - I got it. - I got it. - Would you let me get the check!?
jokes.setup: Polite check-grabbing ritual
jokes.punchline: Escalates to shouting frustration
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation gag but efficiently executed within the Seinfeld show context.

jokes.text: Forgot to go to the cash machine.
jokes.setup: Just fought to pay the check
jokes.punchline: Has no money anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - social bluster hiding practical incompetence. Great economy.

jokes.text: He doesn't have groat's... Oh, that's great. That's good news.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Michael's negative test results
jokes.punchline: Larry's unfortunate word choice given the context
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that works but isn't particularly sophisticated or memorable.

jokes.text: What is he, george clooney? I mean, come on.
jokes.setup: Jason Alexander has tinted windows for celebrity privacy
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to actual A-list movie star
jokes.explanation: Good deflating of Jason's celebrity pretensions, though the setup is a bit obvious.

jokes.text: Boy, you know a lot about this guy. We spend a lot of time together just talking about nothing between takes.
jokes.setup: Cheryl knows detailed info about Jason's car habits
jokes.punchline: Admits to intimate conversations, fueling Larry's suspicion
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Larry's jealousy arc - innocent explanation that sounds suspicious.

jokes.text: I can't. Whenever I make a move you see it a mile away. It's like a poker tell.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggests Jason make a move on Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Jason's moves are transparently obvious
jokes.explanation: Great character insight - Jason's neurotic transparency perfectly captured with poker metaphor.

jokes.text: You realize all your dreams are coming true? Your whole plan is coming to fruition.
jokes.setup: Jason is succeeding with Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Jerry points out the meta-level success of Larry's scheme
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on Larry's scheme working too well, with ironic undertone of backfiring.

jokes.text: Raise your kids without raising them.
jokes.setup: Mocking Jason's book title 'Acting Without Acting'
jokes.punchline: Extends the paradoxical 'without' construction to parenting
jokes.explanation: Clever extension of the absurd book title logic. Very quotable and economical.

jokes.text: Having said that, let me say this... So what is that? So you win either way. A comedian goes up on stage, 'you people are a bunch of morons. Having said that, i'm very happy to be here.'
jokes.setup: Larry notices people using 'having said that' to negate statements
jokes.punchline: Comedian example perfectly illustrates the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational comedy about linguistic contradiction with perfect illustrative example.

jokes.text: I asked him to do me a favor. I didn't say I want to hire you. A favor implies no tip.
jokes.setup: Mocha Joe expects tip for favor
jokes.punchline: Larry's rigid semantic distinction about favors vs. services
jokes.explanation: Pure Larry David logic - technically correct but socially obtuse semantic argument.

jokes.text: Having said that, I think this is a terrific title.
jokes.setup: Just criticized the 'having said that' phrase extensively
jokes.punchline: Immediately uses it himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback - Larry falls into the exact trap he just identified.

jokes.text: Has it ever occurred to you to use a coaster?
jokes.setup: Julia discovers ring stain on family heirloom table
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive accusation delivered as helpful suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the ring stain arc, though the passive-aggression is fairly standard.

jokes.text: It makes me feel comfortable and secure and I don't have to shake hands.
jokes.setup: Larry explains why he always holds his glass
jokes.punchline: Social anxiety manifesting as anti-handshake strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - practical neurosis disguised as social behavior.

jokes.text: Having said that, I don't really think I'm buying your line of bullshit.
jokes.setup: Julia says she believes Larry about the glass
jokes.punchline: Uses 'having said that' to completely contradict herself with hostility
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - Julia weaponizes the phrase Larry just criticized, with perfect hostility.

jokes.text: You would do me a favor and we're even. What's the favor? Go into west hollywood to stern bros. Coffee and pick up my coffee order for tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Larry offers to do Mocha Joe a favor to make things even
jokes.punchline: The 'favor' is an unreasonable long-distance errand
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the favor war, though the punchline is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: No going back in time. You said you would do me a favor. Now do me a favor.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to tip instead of doing the favor
jokes.punchline: Mocha Joe holds Larry to his exact words
jokes.explanation: Mocha Joe outmaneuvers Larry using his own logic against him.

jokes.text: No going back in time. You said you would do me a favor. Now do me a favor.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to pay instead of doing the favor
jokes.punchline: Mocha Joe's inflexible adherence to the exact terms
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mocha Joe's stubborn principles trumping practical solutions.

jokes.text: God damn it!
jokes.setup: Larry realizes he's trapped into doing the Hollywood errand
jokes.punchline: Pure frustrated outburst
jokes.explanation: Standard frustration beat but earned by the escalating situation.

jokes.text: What are you doing? you're picking up beans? It's a long story. I'm gonna be like an hour late.
jokes.setup: Larry has to explain why he's late for their date
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of being late to pick up coffee beans
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment as Larry's trivial obligation ruins his romantic opportunity.

jokes.text: fucking mocha joe!
jokes.setup: Larry's romantic evening gets cancelled because of the bean errand
jokes.punchline: Explosive blame directed at Mocha Joe
jokes.explanation: Cathartic release that caps the escalating frustration, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: Cheryl calls me up, she says come on over. i go over, we worked for like two hours.
jokes.setup: Jason explaining his evening with Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Innocent work session that sounds suspicious to jealous Larry
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's paranoia - innocent explanation that fuels suspicion.

jokes.text: I have known you since 1983. I know when you are lying. And right now you're doing that thing with your mouth when it's like...
jokes.setup: Julia confronts Larry about the ring stain
jokes.punchline: Claims to recognize his specific lying tell from decades of friendship
jokes.explanation: Great character moment using their real friendship history to create fictional conflict.

jokes.text: I will find out and I will drag that person by the hair and bring him into your dressing room soon.
jokes.setup: Julia threatens to find the real culprit
jokes.punchline: Cartoonishly violent threat over a water stain
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but the violent imagery is somewhat over the top for the offense.

jokes.text: Wait, is that in there? I know, but I don't say I made it. No, I say I made it. I know, it was so convincing. I'm playing george.
jokes.setup: Confusion about script vs improvisation
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on Jason's convincing delivery as George
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment about the show-within-show, though somewhat inside baseball.

jokes.text: This is like the greatest relationship I've ever seen you have on the show. I know, george has never been this happy. I'm watching richard benjamin and paula prentiss here.
jokes.setup: Larry commenting on George's relationship success
jokes.punchline: Reference to real married acting couple from the 70s
jokes.explanation: Clever reference that works on multiple levels but may be too obscure for some viewers.

jokes.text: An attempt is not a favor. What? It is a favor. It is a favor. That's as good as a favor. The effort was there.
jokes.setup: Larry claims attempting the favor counts as doing it
jokes.punchline: Semantic argument about effort vs results
jokes.explanation: Pure Larry David logic - technically wrong but argued with complete conviction.

jokes.text: Whatever happened to e for effort, jerry? E for effort! You people think about that. F for favor! C for coffee.
jokes.setup: Larry's frustrated defense of his failed errand
jokes.punchline: Escalates into random letter-association breakdown
jokes.explanation: Great escalation into absurdist territory - Larry's argument completely breaks down into alphabet soup.

jokes.text: They could be eating burritos behind those tinted windows at lunch. That's your proof? They've got tinted windows?
jokes.setup: Larry's paranoia about Jason and Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Burritos as evidence of infidelity, Jerry's incredulous response
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist paranoia - innocent lunch becomes evidence of betrayal in Larry's mind.

jokes.text: I respect wood so much that if I had a piece of wood in my hand right now i'd beat the shit out of you with it, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry questions Susie's respect for wood
jokes.punchline: Violent threat that ironically proves disrespect for wood
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with perfect irony - claiming wood respect while threatening to use it as weapon. Classic Susie explosion.

jokes.text: You said beans and clearly you were busy. You want to think I was busy with beans, go ahead and think that.
jokes.setup: Cheryl interprets Larry's bean errand as being 'busy with beans'
jokes.punchline: Larry's frustrated acceptance of the absurd phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the bean situation with Larry's characteristic defeat in the face of semantic confusion.

jokes.text: This is all your fault, mocha joe. Everything. You busied me with your beans.
jokes.setup: Larry blames Mocha Joe for his problems
jokes.punchline: Adopts Cheryl's 'busied with beans' phrasing to assign blame
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Larry internalizing the absurd phrasing to weaponize it against Mocha Joe.

jokes.text: Why? It's kind of quirky that he's saying this stuff from the bathroom. Who else would do that?
jokes.setup: Larry tries to justify putting Jason in the bathroom for the scene
jokes.punchline: Weak defense of obviously jealous sabotage
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Larry's transparent sabotage is called out, though the setup is obvious.

jokes.text: Like twins in a womb, right? That's how we did this show.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing their intimate creative partnership
jokes.punchline: Visceral metaphor for creative collaboration
jokes.explanation: Vivid and slightly unsettling metaphor that captures their creative intimacy perfectly.

jokes.text: Cheryl! aha! no! Mocha joe! mocha joe! Somebody help me! Oh shit! Oh no! ow! Oh no!
jokes.setup: Larry investigating Jason's car with tinted windows
jokes.punchline: Gets attacked by Jason's dogs, calling for help
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy payoff for Larry's snooping, though the setup makes it somewhat earned.

jokes.text: They're going to destroy the dogs now because mocha joe was bitten. Really? Yes.
jokes.setup: Discussion of consequences from dog bite
jokes.punchline: The dogs will be put down because of Larry's actions
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation where Larry's paranoia has serious consequences for innocent animals.

jokes.text: Then you go to a showroom. Go to a car dealer and check it out. Talk to a salesman.
jokes.setup: Larry claims he was checking headroom in Jason's car
jokes.punchline: Jerry's logical suggestion for car shopping
jokes.explanation: Jerry's deadpan response exposes the absurdity of Larry's excuse.

jokes.text: That's between the coffee and the wood.
jokes.setup: Larry asks how coffee stain got on Mocha Joe's table
jokes.punchline: Philosophical non-answer treating it as natural mystery
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd deadpan response with perfect economy - treats obvious cause as mystical interaction.

jokes.text: This is a low-grade wood. Oh, so you discriminate amongst wood? I guess you could say that.
jokes.setup: Mocha Joe explains his ring stain
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous response to wood discrimination
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the wood respect concept to absurd discrimination.

jokes.text: So you go to a funeral, you're more upset about the coffin than the deceased?
jokes.setup: Larry cares more about wood stains than dog lives
jokes.punchline: Extreme hypothetical exposing Larry's misplaced priorities
jokes.explanation: Devastating escalation that perfectly captures Larry's skewed priorities with vivid hypothetical.

jokes.text: Oh, larry david, wood detective.
jokes.setup: Larry's obsession with finding ring stain culprits
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic title that reduces Larry's investigation to absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflating nickname that captures Larry's obsessive investigation in three words.

jokes.text: Those dogs are getting gassed. I'd do it myself if I could. And I'd have it televised.
jokes.setup: Mocha Joe threatens to press charges against the dogs
jokes.punchline: Escalates to wanting to personally execute them on TV
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that shows Mocha Joe's vindictive nature, though the imagery is quite harsh.

jokes.text: Is there like another word for solid? Fav... I can't hear. What, larry? Favor.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to avoid saying 'favor'
jokes.punchline: Mocha Joe forces him to say the word
jokes.explanation: Cringe moment where Larry can't escape the word that trapped him.

jokes.text: Did you say favor, larry? Does this favor involve a tip?
jokes.setup: Larry tries to ask for another favor
jokes.punchline: Mocha Joe immediately turns it back to the tipping issue
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Mocha Joe learned his lesson about Larry's semantic games.

jokes.text: That's for me bringing the jumper cables to your office. That's for not picking up my fucking coffee beans. One dog lives.
jokes.setup: Larry pays two different amounts
jokes.punchline: Mocha Joe's transactional approach to dog lives
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy reducing dog lives to a financial transaction.

jokes.text: scampering and scurrying, I see you! What am I doing? What are you doing?
jokes.setup: Larry catches Jason hiding from him
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic description of Jason's evasion
jokes.explanation: Larry's paranoid language escalates the awkward confrontation.

jokes.text: I'm george. You're not attracted to him, you're attracted to me. I wrote that stuff. I'm george.
jokes.setup: Larry confronts Cheryl about her attraction to Jason
jokes.punchline: Claims credit for George's personality as his own creation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment where Larry claims ownership of the George character he created. Peak cringe and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: It was so lame. Who's gonna buy that? They get together... We don't do endings like that. You know it's fantasy crap.
jokes.setup: Larry justifies changing the Seinfeld reunion ending
jokes.punchline: Dismisses happy endings as unrealistic for their show
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on Seinfeld's cynical worldview, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Larry, we already screwed up one finale. We can't do another.
jokes.setup: Larry wants to change the reunion show ending
jokes.punchline: Reference to the controversial original Seinfeld finale
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-reference to real criticism of the Seinfeld finale, self-deprecating and clever.

jokes.text: How do you even have the show? What do you have? You have a three-legged goat here.
jokes.setup: Discussion of doing the show without Jason
jokes.punchline: Jerry's animal metaphor for an incomplete cast
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor for the dysfunction of an incomplete ensemble cast.

jokes.text: There were two darrens on 'bewitched.' Nobody liked that second darren.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests he could replace Jason as George
jokes.punchline: Classic TV reference about failed recasting
jokes.explanation: Classic TV reference that perfectly illustrates why Larry can't replace Jason, though somewhat obvious.

jokes.text: There's no john, paul, george and larry.
jokes.setup: Larry wants to join the iconic Seinfeld cast
jokes.punchline: Beatles reference showing Larry doesn't belong
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Beatles reference with perfect economy - shows iconic status and Larry's outsider position.

jokes.text: George is getting upset.
jokes.setup: Larry attempting to play George in the reunion
jokes.punchline: Classic George catchphrase delivered by Larry
jokes.explanation: Classic callback to original series, though not particularly original as a joke itself.

jokes.text: The itoilet. And I thought of it. That was one killer app.
jokes.setup: Kramer describes his iPhone app idea for finding toilets
jokes.punchline: Proudly names it with obvious toilet pun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer invention with predictable but fitting name. Character-appropriate enthusiasm.

jokes.text: An iphone application that leads you via your g.p.s. To the nearest acceptable toilet wherever you are in the world. The itoilet.
jokes.setup: Kramer describes his lost business idea
jokes.punchline: The app name 'itoilet'
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer absurd business idea with perfect character integration and memorable name.

jokes.text: A few bad strangers that ruined it for the rest of us.
jokes.setup: Discussion of why we can't meet new people
jokes.punchline: Blames 'bad strangers' for ruining stranger reputation
jokes.explanation: Great observational comedy with perfect Jerry delivery style and logic.

jokes.text: Pretty pretty pretty pretty pretty good.
jokes.setup: George asked how he is
jokes.punchline: Larry's signature catchphrase delivered as George
jokes.explanation: Iconic Larry David catchphrase cross-pollinating into Seinfeld world. Pure fan service but effective.

jokes.text: Dating? Just sex.
jokes.setup: Asked if she's dating someone she's meeting
jokes.punchline: Her blunt, matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing - the blunt honesty subverts expectation beautifully.

jokes.text: Dating? Just sex. She is easy to talk to.
jokes.setup: Asked if she's dating someone new
jokes.punchline: Casually reduces relationship to just sex, Jerry's understated reaction
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable moment with Jerry's perfect deadpan reaction comment.

jokes.text: You called his book a pamphlet? It's so thin. It is, it's very thin. It's not a book. I know it's not a book.
jokes.setup: Larry explains his fight with Jason
jokes.punchline: Doubles down on book/pamphlet criticism with Cheryl's agreement
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that reinforces Larry's stubborn position with support from unexpected ally.

jokes.text: This could be the news that finally kills her. Because of the shock or because she doesn't like me? i'm fine either way.
jokes.setup: George will call his mother about getting back together
jokes.punchline: Amanda's darkly pragmatic response about his mother's death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about maternal disapproval with perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Tell her that sometimes you have to be away from something to really appreciate it. That's how I feel about cuban food.
jokes.setup: What to tell mother about getting back together
jokes.punchline: Compares relationship to Cuban food ambivalence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comparison that captures ambivalent feelings perfectly. Very George-like twisted logic.

jokes.text: That's how I feel about cuban food. When I'm eating it I don't really like it. When I'm away for a little while I Miss it.
jokes.setup: Discussing appreciation after absence
jokes.punchline: George's odd analogy to Cuban food
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific George analogy that perfectly captures his thought process.

jokes.text: Get out. That's recidivism. It's the same thing I saw last night on 'prison lockdown.'
jokes.setup: George and Amanda getting back together
jokes.punchline: Compares relationship reunion to criminal recidivism via prison show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer logic connecting romance to criminal justice through TV obsession. Brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: See, that's another ponzi scheme.
jokes.setup: Pre-nup prevents George from getting his own money back
jokes.punchline: Compares marriage contract to financial fraud
jokes.explanation: Clever callback tying marriage dynamics to the episode's Madoff theme with sharp observation.

jokes.text: Do you respect wood? I... Guess so. You guess so? You don't know if you respect wood?
jokes.setup: Amanda makes ring stain on Larry's table
jokes.punchline: Larry's obsessive wood respect interrogation of innocent victim
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Larry's obsession has reached peak absurdity with aggressive interrogation.

jokes.text: Do you respect wood? I... Guess so. You guess so? You don't know if you respect wood?
jokes.setup: Larry questions Amanda about wood respect
jokes.punchline: His incredulous response to her uncertainty
jokes.explanation: Larry's wood obsession reaches its absurd peak with this earnest interrogation.

jokes.text: Having said that, I would love for you to call julia and tell her that you left the ring stain.
jokes.setup: Larry discovers Amanda is the real culprit
jokes.punchline: Uses the phrase he's been mocking while asking for vindication
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback convergence - Larry uses his pet peeve phrase while solving the ring stain mystery.

jokes.text: Having said that, I would love for you to call julia and tell her that you left the ring stain.
jokes.setup: Larry found the real ring stain culprit
jokes.punchline: Uses the 'having said that' phrase he previously mocked
jokes.explanation: Perfect final callback to the 'having said that' theme, showing Larry's hypocrisy in moment of victory.
